comments.body: It wasn't immediately obvious to me what the failure condition here is, and I am not sure we've seen this happen before, so it may represent a rare race?  I think this is inside a systemd unit, but I didn't dig in to check.
Can you reproduce this somehow or did this happen just once?

Looking at the code we assume the we always have a valid net.Listener returned by activation.Listeners()
https://github.com/coreos/go-systemd/blob/f5f8d59a116b1ca501dd87cdb28f6ce89ccf58dc/activation/listeners.go#L25-L28
However it can return nil if it cannot listen on the fd, i.e. udp connection so we should check that in podman.
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13950 should fix the panic but I have no idea how to trigger this condition. Maybe systemd used an invalid fd for podman?

> Can you reproduce this somehow or did this happen just once?

This is the only time I have seen this happen.  I wouldn't worry too much more about this.
